intro: Stachystemon polyandrus, also known as the Australian fingered orchid, is a flowering plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
